name: Victoria Hurley
strapLine3: for building polished, accessible web applications.
intro1: My journey into software began with a fascination for how complex systems are built from the ground up. Before I was architecting digital interfaces, I was a luthier with a First Class Honours in Music Technology with a focus on guitar making. Whether carving a fretboard or refactoring a reactive Angular component, my approach remains the same: a deep respect for precision, durability, and the user’s experience with the final 'instrument'.
intro2: For the past four years, I have been an embedded consultant on the British Airways account , contributing to high-traffic re-platforming projects across major framework evolutions. This includes a recent upgrade from Angular 15 to 19 for the Disruptions team and a migration to Angular 16 and Nest.js for the Seat Map. A key highlight was leading the end-to-end accessibility transformation for the Seat Map. Working independently from audit reports, I performed the complex technical remediation required to achieve full WCAG compliance while delivering essential self-service solutions for passengers.

experiences.title: Software engineer
description: Since joining Leighton, I have been consistently embedded within British Airways’ digital ecosystem. Working in a high-pressure, enterprise environment, I’ve contributed to the modernization of guest-facing services - transitioning legacy systems into high-performance Angular applications. My role has evolved from core frontend development to taking increasing ownership of the full stack, including Nest.js backend services and AWS/Terraform infrastructure. A major focus of my tenure has been ensuring digital inclusivity by leading accessibility remediation projects to meet strict WCAG standards.

experiences.title: Software engineer
description: At REG, I played a versatile role in the evolution of a complex, subscription-based platform serving the regulated insurance sector. My work shifted with the product's highest-priority needs, ranging from architecting interactive data dashboards for compliance tracking to taking over the International Regulators project from the Principal Engineer. In addition to my engineering responsibilities, I served as the sole designer for the team, utilizing Adobe XD and Illustrator to bridge the gap between regulatory requirements and a seamless user experience. This dual-focus allowed me to drive both technical component reusability and UI/UX consistency across the entire platform.

projects.title: Reactive MEAN Stack: Signal-Based Task Management
projects.description: An exploration of modern Angular reactivity, replacing traditional Change Detection with Signal-based state. This MEAN stack application demonstrates full CRUD capabilities alongside a dedicated Server-Status utility, showcasing cross-stack health checks and responsive data-fetching patterns.

buildInfo.intro: A Full-Stack application built with Angular 21 and Node.js. Hosted on GitHub Pages and Render.
